info.title: PMXT Hosted Router API
info.description: Hosted-only endpoints for cross-venue search, matching, arbitrage, and SQL.

tags.name: Trading (Hosted)
tags.description: Hosted trading endpoints. Build / submit / cancel orders via `trade.pmxt.dev/v0/*`. Hosted-mode is the default when `pmxt_api_key` is set.

tags.name: Orders & Positions (Hosted)
tags.description: Hosted account reads. Open orders, fills, balances, and positions via `trade.pmxt.dev/v0/*`. Requires `pmxt_api_key` + `wallet_address`.

tags.name: MatchedMarkets
tags.description: Cross-venue matched market and event clusters.

tags.name: SQL
tags.description: Direct read-only SQL access to the catalog (Enterprise).

post.description:
Construct an unsigned order for Polymarket or Opinion so the caller can inspect it before signing. Returns the EIP-712 typed-data payload, the resolved catalog identifiers, and a `built_order_id` to pass to `submitOrderHosted` after signing locally. Useful when you want to display order details to an end user before they approve the signature — for one-shot "just place the order" usage, call `createOrderHosted` instead.

Identify the target outcome by EITHER (a) catalog `outcome_id` UUID (canonical, cross-venue) OR (b) `venue` + `venue_outcome_id` (the venue-native id returned by `client.fetch_markets()` on a venue client). Both shapes are accepted; the SDK picks the right one automatically based on what you pass in.

See the [signing guide](/guides/signing) for the wallet flow.

200.description: Built order with typed data to sign.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

403.description: Insufficient escrow balance to back the requested order size.

404.description: Outcome not found in the catalog.

422.description: Invalid order parameters (e.g. price out of range, denom mismatch).

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# All hosted venues are funded once on Polygon — see /guides/escrow-lifecycle.
# Hosted writes need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address + private_key (signs EIP-712 locally).
# The SDK accepts either a catalog UUID or a venue-native id from client.fetch_markets().
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",    # EVM address — its Polygon USDC funds the escrow
    private_key="0x...",    # any EVM key controlling that address
)
market = client.fetch_markets({"query": "trump 2028"})[0]
yes = next(o for o in market.outcomes if o.label.lower() == "yes")
order = client.build_order(
    outcome=yes,
    side="buy",
    type="limit",
    amount=10,
    price=0.55,
)
print(order.id, order.status)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket

x-codeSamples.label: Opinion

x-mint.content:
<Info>
  **Available on:** Polymarket, Opinion. Other venues raise `NotSupported` in hosted mode — for those, run a [local PMXT service](/guides/self-hosted).
</Info>

<Note>
  **Before your first call:** fund your PreFundedEscrow with USDC (one-time per venue). See [escrow lifecycle](/guides/escrow-lifecycle).
</Note>

<Tip>
  Use any EVM private key. PMXT custodies USDC on Polygon for every hosted venue — including Opinion (BSC), which PMXT settles cross-chain for you.
</Tip>

post.description:
Submit a signed order to Polymarket or Opinion and return the resulting `Order` with id, fill status, average price, and on-chain tx hash. The `built_order_id` must come from a recent `buildOrderHosted` call and be submitted before its expiry.

For most callers, prefer `createOrderHosted`, which builds, signs, and submits in a single call.

200.description: Order accepted by the hosted backend.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

410.description: `built_order_id` expired before submission. Re-run `buildOrderHosted` and submit again.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# All hosted venues are funded once on Polygon — see /guides/escrow-lifecycle.
# Hosted writes need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address + private_key (signs EIP-712 locally).
# No catalog UUID lookup required — venue-native ids from fetch_markets() work directly.
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",    # EVM address — its Polygon USDC funds the escrow
    private_key="0x...",    # any EVM key controlling that address
)
market = client.fetch_markets({"query": "trump 2028"})[0]
built = client.build_order(
    outcome=market.yes,
    side="buy",
    type="limit",
    amount=10,
    price=0.55,
)
order = client.submit_order(built)
print(order.id, order.status)

post.description:
Place a limit or market order on Polymarket or Opinion in one call. The SDK builds the EIP-712 order payload, signs it locally with your `private_key`, and submits it against your [PreFundedEscrow](/guides/escrow-lifecycle) balance. Returns the resulting `Order` — id, fill status, average fill price, fees, and the on-chain settlement tx hash once it lands.

Identify the target outcome by EITHER (a) catalog `outcome_id` UUID (canonical, cross-venue) OR (b) `venue` + `venue_outcome_id` (the venue-native id returned by `client.fetch_markets()` on a venue client such as `pmxt.Polymarket`). The natural workflow — `market = client.fetch_markets(...)[0]; client.create_order(outcome=market.yes, ...)` — works without any catalog UUID lookup.

Use `buildOrderHosted` + `submitOrderHosted` separately only when you need to inspect or display the order payload before signing (e.g. a custodial flow where a human approves each trade).

200.description: Order accepted by the hosted backend.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# All hosted venues are funded once on Polygon — see /guides/escrow-lifecycle.
# Hosted writes need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address + private_key (signs EIP-712 locally).
# The SDK accepts either a catalog UUID or a venue-native id from client.fetch_markets().
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",    # EVM address — its Polygon USDC funds the escrow
    private_key="0x...",    # any EVM key controlling that address
)
market = client.fetch_markets({"query": "trump 2028"})[0]
yes = next(o for o in market.outcomes if o.label.lower() == "yes")
order = client.create_order(
    outcome=yes,
    side="buy",
    type="limit",
    amount=10,
    price=0.55,
)
print(order.id, order.status)

post.description:
Build the unsigned cancel payload for an existing open order on Polymarket or Opinion. Returns the EIP-712 typed-data to sign locally plus a `cancel_id` you submit to `POST /v0/orders/cancel` once signed.

The SDK's `cancelOrder()` chains both steps — call this endpoint directly only if you need to show the cancel payload to an end user before signing.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# All hosted venues are funded once on Polygon — see /guides/escrow-lifecycle.
# Hosted writes need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address + private_key (signs EIP-712 locally).
# outcome_id is a catalog UUID from client.fetch_events() — not a venue-native ID.
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",    # EVM address — its Polygon USDC funds the escrow
    private_key="0x...",    # any EVM key controlling that address
)
cancelled = client.cancel_order("order_abc123")
print(cancelled.id, cancelled.status)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
Opinion

    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",    # EVM address — its Polygon USDC funds the escrow
    private_key="0x...",    # any EVM key controlling that address
)
cancelled = client.cancel_order("order_abc123")
print(cancelled.id, cancelled.status)

get.description: Check the USDC available for trading on Polymarket and Opinion for `address`. Returns the balance held inside the PMXT [PreFundedEscrow](/guides/escrow-lifecycle) contract on behalf of the wallet — *not* the venue-native CLOB-proxy balance. Top up by sending USDC to the escrow.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

503.description: Catalog or escrow indexer temporarily unavailable.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
balance = client.fetch_balance()
print(balance.amount, balance.currency)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket

get.description: List the open positions held by `address` across Polymarket and Opinion. Each position carries the catalog `market_id` / `outcome_id`, size, average entry price, and — when available — the current mark price and unrealized PnL.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
for position in client.fetch_positions():
    print(position.market_id, position.shares, position.current_value)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket

get.description: List the resting limit orders placed by `address` that have not yet filled or been cancelled. Each entry includes the catalog `market_id` / `outcome_id`, side, size, price, and time-in-force. Optionally filter by `venue` to scope to Polymarket or Opinion only.

parameters.name: venue
parameters.description: Restrict to a single hosted venue.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
for order in client.fetch_open_orders():
    print(order.id, order.side, order.price, order.remaining)

get.description:
List historical fills for `address` on Polymarket and Opinion. Each trade carries the executed price, size, fees, and the on-chain settlement tx hash.

In hosted mode, closed orders are modelled as trades — use this endpoint instead of `fetchClosedOrders` / `fetchAllOrders` (which raise `NotSupported`).

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
for trade in client.fetch_my_trades():
    print(trade.id, trade.side, trade.amount, trade.price, trade.tx_hash)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket

get.description: Look up a single order on Polymarket or Opinion by its `order_id` (the same id returned from `createOrderHosted` or `fetchOpenOrdersHosted`). Returns the order's current status, filled / remaining size, average fill price, and — once settled — the on-chain tx hash.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
order = client.fetch_order("order_abc123")
print(order.id, order.status, order.filled, order.remaining)

x-mint.content:
<Info>
  **Available on:** Polymarket, Opinion. Other venues raise `NotSupported` in hosted mode — for those, run a [local PMXT service](/guides/self-hosted).
</Info>

<Tip>
  Use any EVM private key. PMXT custodies USDC on Polygon for every hosted venue — including Opinion (BSC), which PMXT settles cross-chain for you.
</Tip>

bearerAuth.description: Required when calling the hosted API directly (curl, requests, fetch). SDK users pass credentials via constructor params instead.

HostedErrorResponse.description: Error envelope returned by `trade.pmxt.dev/v0/*` endpoints. Mirrors the `ErrorDetail` shape used inside `BaseResponse.error` from the sidecar spec.

BuildOrderHostedRequest.description: Hosted build-order request. The caller must identify the target outcome in one of two ways: either (a) pass a catalog `outcome_id` UUID, or (b) pass `venue` plus `venue_outcome_id` (the venue-native identifier returned by `client.fetch_markets()` on a venue client such as `pmxt.Polymarket` or `pmxt.Opinion`). `market_id` is optional in both cases -- the backend derives it from the supplied identifier when omitted.

market_id.description: Catalog market UUID. Optional -- derived from `outcome_id` or `(venue, venue_outcome_id)` when omitted.

outcome_id.description: Catalog outcome UUID. Provide this OR `(venue, venue_outcome_id)`.

venue.description: Venue name. Required when identifying the outcome via `venue_outcome_id` instead of the catalog `outcome_id` UUID.

venue_outcome_id.description: Venue-native outcome identifier (e.g. Polymarket `tokenId`, Opinion outcome hash). Required when `venue` is supplied instead of `outcome_id`.

amount.description: Order size. For `market` buys, in USDC; for `market` sells / `limit`, in shares.

user_address.description: EVM wallet address that will sign the resulting typed data.

BuildOrderHostedResponse.description: Hosted build-order response. The caller must sign `typed_data` locally (and `pull_typed_data` if present) and POST the signatures back via `submitOrderHosted` before the order expires.

built_order_id.description: Opaque server-side key used by `submitOrderHosted` to look up the build context.

typed_data.description: EIP-712 typed data for the order. Sign locally with the wallet that matches `user_address`.

pull_typed_data.description: Optional secondary EIP-712 payload for venues that require a pull-authorization (e.g. Polymarket neg-risk markets).

quote.description: Pre-trade quote: expected average fill price, slippage, and fees.

resolved.description: Venue-native fields resolved from the catalog UUIDs (`venue`, `token_id`, `neg_risk`, `tick_size`).

SubmitOrderHostedRequest.description: Hosted submit-order request. `signature` is the local EIP-712 signature over `BuildOrderHostedResponse.typed_data`.

pull_signature.description: Signature for the secondary pull-authorization, required when `pull_typed_data` was returned.

wait.description: When `true`, the server waits for on-chain settlement before responding.

order_id.description: Unified order id, as returned by `fetchOpenOrdersHosted`.

deadline.description: Unix epoch (s) after which the cancel build expires.

OrderV0.description: Hosted-mode `Order` shape. Mirrors `pmxt.Order` so the SDK can return it directly. `tx_hash`, `chain`, and `block_number` populate once execution settles on-chain.

tx_hash.description: On-chain transaction hash. Null until settled.

PositionV0.description: Hosted-mode `Position` shape. `current_price`, `current_value`, `entry_price`, `realized_pnl`, and `outcome_label` may be null when the server doesn't yet have the data.

BalanceV0.description: Hosted-mode `Balance` shape. Returns USDC held inside the PMXT `PreFundedEscrow` contract.

venue.description: Set when the hosted backend returns a per-venue breakdown; null when balance is venue-agnostic.
